content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a game, which he starts with a score of S=0S = 0S=0. He also has an integer NNN.

In one move, Chef does the following:


Uniformly randomly pick an integer XXX between 000 and 2N‚àí12^N - 12N‚àí1 (inclusive of both ends)
Update his score as S‚ÜíS‚à£XS \rightarrow S \mid XS‚ÜíS‚à£X, where ‚à£\mid‚à£ denotes the bitwise OR operation
For example, if Chef's current score is S=6S = 6S=6 and he picks X=10X = 10X=10, his new score is 6‚à£10=146\mid 10 = 146‚à£10=14.

Chef stops when his score SSS becomes equal to 2N‚àí12^N-12N‚àí1.  What is the expected number of moves Chef performs?

Output the answer modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7. That is, the answer can be expressed as a fraction P/QP/QP/Q, where gcd‚Å°(Q,109+7)=1\gcd(Q, 10^9 + 7) = 1gcd(Q,109+7)=1. Print P‚ãÖQ‚àí1(mod 109+7)P\cdot Q^{-1} \pmod{\ 10^9 + 7}P‚ãÖQ‚àí1(mod 109+7), where Q‚àí1Q^{-1}Q‚àí1 denotes the modular multiplicative inverse of QQQ with respect to 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input contains an integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the expected number of moves Chef performs, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases do not exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105
Sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 10310^3103

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: In each move, Chef chooses either 000 or 111, each with a probability of 1/21/21/2. The game ends as soon as Chef chooses 111. Thus, the probability that the game takes exactly kkk moves is 2‚àík2^{-k}2‚àík.
The required expected value is then ‚àëk=1‚àûk‚ãÖ2‚àík\displaystyle\sum_{k=1}^\infty k\cdot 2^{-k}k=1‚àë‚àû‚Äãk‚ãÖ2‚àík, which equals 222.
Test case 222: In each move, Chef chooses an integer in [0,3][0, 3][0,3]. The answer is 8/38/38/3.
